description: Machine-readable validation rule definitions for cross-skills validator

rules.title: Required frontmatter fields present
rules.description: Skill .md files require 'name' and 'description'. Cursor .mdc files require 'description' and 'alwaysApply'. UIR requires 'meta', 'context', 'rules'.
rules.fix: Add the missing frontmatter field to the file header.

rules.title: YAML frontmatter is valid YAML
rules.description: The --- delimited block at the top of the file must parse as valid YAML.
rules.fix: Check for unquoted colons, tabs instead of spaces, or invalid boolean/null values.

rules.title: UIR version is valid semver
rules.description: meta.version must match the pattern X.Y.Z where X, Y, Z are non-negative integers.
rules.fix: Set meta.version to a semver string, e.g. '1.0.0'.

rules.title: UIR enum fields use allowed values
rules.description: Fields with enum constraints (meta.source.method, rules[].scope, rules[].category, hooks[].event) must use values defined in the UIR schema.
rules.fix: Check schema/uir.schema.json for the allowed enum values for each field.

rules.title: Cursor alwaysApply is a boolean
rules.description: Cursor parses alwaysApply as a boolean. Using a string 'true' or 'false' will be treated as truthy/falsy string, not a boolean.
rules.fix: Use 'alwaysApply: true' or 'alwaysApply: false' without quotes.

rules.title: UIR project_summary is substantive
rules.description: context.project_summary under 50 characters likely has an unfilled placeholder.
rules.fix: Write a meaningful project summary of at least 1-2 sentences.

rules.title: UIR rules array is non-empty
rules.description: A UIR with no rules will produce configs with no behavioral guidance for AI assistants.
rules.fix: Add at least a few fundamental rules reflecting your project's conventions.

rules.title: memory.always_load paths exist
rules.description: Every path in memory.always_load[] must resolve to an existing file relative to the repo root.
rules.fix: Verify the file paths or remove non-existent entries from memory.always_load.

rules.title: memory.on_demand paths exist
rules.description: Every path in memory.on_demand[] must resolve to an existing file.
rules.fix: Verify the file paths or remove non-existent entries from memory.on_demand.

rules.title: rules[].id values are unique
rules.description: Every rule must have a unique id within the UIR document.
rules.fix: Rename duplicate rule ids to be unique slugs.

rules.title: agents[].id values are unique
rules.description: Every agent must have a unique id within the UIR document.
rules.fix: Rename duplicate agent ids.

rules.title: skills[].id values are unique
rules.description: Every skill must have a unique id within the UIR document.
rules.fix: Rename duplicate skill ids.

rules.title: Template variable references resolve
rules.description: Every {{VARIABLE}} placeholder in a .tmpl file must correspond to a known UIR field or standard template variable.
rules.fix: Remove or fill in unknown placeholders, or add them to the template variable substitution table in modules/generator.md.

rules.title: CLAUDE.md @import paths exist
rules.description: Every @path/to/file reference in CLAUDE.md must resolve to an existing file.
rules.fix: Create the referenced file or remove the @import reference.

rules.title: Potentially contradictory rules
rules.description: Two rules with the same scope and scope_value contain opposite instructions (e.g. 'always use X' vs 'never use X').
rules.fix: Review the flagged rules and consolidate them into a single authoritative rule.

rules.title: Pattern in both allow and deny
rules.description: A permission pattern that appears in both permissions.allow and permissions.deny creates undefined behavior. In Claude Code, deny takes precedence.
rules.fix: Remove the pattern from one of the lists. If deny is intended, remove from allow.

rules.title: File in both always_load and ignore
rules.description: A file cannot be both always loaded for context and ignored.
rules.fix: Remove the file from either memory.always_load or memory.ignore.

rules.title: Same rule id in UIR and extensions
rules.description: An extensions.* object contains a rule id that already exists in the top-level rules array. The extension will overwrite the UIR version.
rules.fix: Use a different id in the extension or remove the top-level rule if the extension is intentionally overriding it.

rules.title: Cursor rule has both alwaysApply: true and globs
rules.description: When alwaysApply is true, the globs field has no effect in Cursor. The rule will apply to all files regardless of globs.
rules.fix: Either remove the globs field, or set alwaysApply: false to use glob-based activation.

rules.title: Cursor glob patterns are valid
rules.description: Cursor uses picomatch glob semantics. Check for unbalanced braces, invalid ** usage, or empty patterns.
rules.fix: Fix the glob pattern. Use ** for recursive matching, * for single path segment.

rules.title: Unreachable Cursor rule
rules.description: A .mdc file with alwaysApply: false, no globs, and no description will never auto-activate. It can only be used as a manual @reference.
rules.fix: Set alwaysApply: true, add globs, add a meaningful description, or document it as a manual-only rule.

rules.title: Hook command references undefined env vars
rules.description: Shell variable references ($VAR or ${VAR}) in hook commands should be defined in the env block or be common system variables.
rules.fix: Add the variable to the settings.json env block or use a hardcoded value.

rules.title: Claude Code permission pattern format
rules.description: Permission patterns should be: bare tool name (Read), ToolName(glob) (Bash(git *)), or ToolName(*) for all invocations.
rules.fix: Use the format: 'ToolName' or 'ToolName(pattern)' where pattern is a glob.

rules.title: Aider model value format
rules.description: The model field in .aider.conf.yml should match a known model identifier. Check the Aider docs for supported model strings.
rules.fix: Use a known model string such as 'claude-sonnet-4-5', 'gpt-4o', or 'gemini-2.0-flash'.

rules.title: .aider.conf.yml is valid YAML
rules.description: The Aider configuration file must be valid YAML.
rules.fix: Fix YAML syntax errors in the configuration file.

rules.title: Two rules have identical content
rules.description: Rules with identical content (after whitespace normalization) are redundant.
rules.fix: Remove the duplicate rule, keeping the one with a more descriptive id and more populated fields.

rules.title: Two rules share the same id
rules.description: Rule ids must be unique. Same as REF-003.
rules.fix: Rename one of the duplicate ids.

rules.title: Same glob in multiple alwaysApply Cursor rules
rules.description: Multiple .mdc files with alwaysApply: true and overlapping globs will both always load for matching files, potentially with conflicting guidance.
rules.fix: Consolidate overlapping rules into a single file or ensure they are complementary, not conflicting.

rules.title: Duplicate ignore patterns
rules.description: The same pattern appears multiple times in ignore_patterns or memory.ignore.
rules.fix: Remove duplicate entries from the ignore lists.

rules.title: CLAUDE.md @import cycle
rules.description: A chain of @import references forms a cycle, which would cause infinite recursion when loading.
rules.fix: Break the import cycle by restructuring the import hierarchy. Extract shared content to a non-importing file.

rules.title: UIR skill cross-reference cycle
rules.description: A skill's instructions reference another skill that references back to the first skill.
rules.fix: Extract shared instructions into a common base section rather than cross-referencing skills.

rules.title: Hooks present but target is not Claude Code
rules.description: UIR hooks are Claude Code-native. When generating for Cursor, they degrade to rule text. For Codex/Aider, they are dropped entirely.
rules.fix: This is expected degradation. Review the CROSS-SKILLS: DEGRADED comments in the generated files.

rules.title: Permissions present but target is not Claude Code
rules.description: Tool permissions are Claude Code-native. They will be converted to rule text in other ecosystems with no enforcement.
rules.fix: This is expected degradation. Review the generated rule text to ensure it communicates the intent clearly.

rules.title: Sub-agents present but target is Cursor or Aider
rules.description: Multi-agent orchestration is not supported in Cursor or Aider. Agents will be flattened to context sections.
rules.fix: This is expected degradation. Consider whether the flattened context adequately conveys the agent roles.

rules.title: File-glob rules will be lost for Codex or Aider
rules.description: Rules with scope: 'file-glob' have no native equivalent in Codex CLI or Aider. They will be flattened to global rules with a scope annotation.
rules.fix: This is expected degradation. The scope context is preserved as text in the flattened rule.

rules.title: Permissions lists are empty
rules.description: Both permissions.allow and permissions.deny are empty. Consider adding explicit tool permission rules for security-sensitive projects.
rules.fix: Add tool permission rules to permissions.allow and permissions.deny as appropriate for your project's security requirements.

rules.title: No skills defined
rules.description: UIR skills[] is empty. Skills enable reusable, invocable task definitions for Claude Code.
rules.fix: Run cross-skills in build-skill mode to generate reusable skill files from the pre-built library or from scratch.
